items.title: Meta breaks ground on a new AI-optimized data center in Tulsa, Oklahoma
items.why_it_matters: Signals continued capex for training/inference capacity—useful context for model cadence and availability.
items.brief: Meta is adding more AI-optimized infrastructure capacity with a new Tulsa data center project.
items.details: Meta announced it is breaking ground on a new AI-optimized data center in Tulsa, Oklahoma. The company frames the build as infrastructure to support growing AI workloads. While not a model release, it’s a concrete indicator of scaling plans for training and inference. Expect this to translate into more headroom for product features and model rollouts over time.

items.title: Amazon invests an additional $5B in Anthropic; outlines up to $20B more tied to milestones
items.why_it_matters: Landmark capital + compute alignment that can materially affect Anthropic’s training scale and product velocity.
items.brief: Amazon adds $5B more into Anthropic (and signals up to $20B additional), deepening the AWS tie-up.
items.details: Amazon announced a new $5 billion investment in Anthropic, with the possibility of up to an additional $20 billion tied to commercial milestones. The post positions the partnership around long-term AWS infrastructure usage and joint go-to-market. For builders, this matters because it can influence where Anthropic’s training/inference capacity lives and how quickly new Claude capabilities ship. It’s one of the larger strategic AI investments disclosed this week.
